goal: Show author and activity time in mainline log output

summary.title: Show author and time in log output
summary.what: Updated mainline log entries to include activity time and author in text output, and added author metadata to log JSON results.
summary.why: Agents need a concise git-log-like view that shows who created or represented each intent and when it was active.
summary.user_goal: Display log rows as intent, status, time, author, title, and thread; record new actor events with a git user based display name while falling back to actor_id for old data.

decisions.chose: Use git config user.name for new event actor_name metadata.
decisions.rationale: This matches the requested scheme A while preserving actor_id as the stable identity key.
rejected: Use only the actor hash for display

decisions.chose: Fallback to actor_id when actor_name is absent.
decisions.rationale: Existing actor logs do not need migration.

decisions.chose: Print local YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM activity time and author before the title.
decisions.rationale: This keeps the output compact and close to the requested example.

rejected.alternative: Rewrite or migrate historical actor logs to include names.
rejected.reason: The requested behavior only needs new events to carry actor_name and old events can fall back to actor_id.

risks: Historical rows still show actor_id until those intents are represented by newer actor-name-aware events.

architectural_claims: Actor display names are optional event metadata while actor_id remains the stable identity key.
behavioral_changes: mainline log text output includes activity time and author before the title.
New sealed and check events include actor_name derived from git user.name when available.
Log JSON entries expose author and actor_name fields.

api_changes.signature: mainline log JSON intent entry author and actor_name fields

data_model_changes.name: BaseEvent.actor_name
data_model_changes.migration_notes: Historical events omit actor_name and use actor_id fallback.

migration_notes: No migration required; historical events without actor_name fall back to actor_id.
